from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.core.handlers.wsgi import WSGIHandler, WSGIRequest, get_script_name
from django.core.signals import request_finished, request_started
from django.db import close_old_connections, connection
from django.test import (
    AsyncRequestFactory,
    TestRequestBuilder,
    SimpleTestCase,
    TransactionTestCase,
    override_settings,
)


class HandlerTests(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def setUp(self):
        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)
        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)

    def test_middleware_initialized(self):
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        self.assertIsNotNone(handler._middleware_chain)

    def test_bad_path_info(self):
        """
        A non-UTF-8 path populates PATH_INFO with an URL-encoded path and
        produces a 404.
        """
        environ = self.request_factory.get("/").environ
        environ["PATH_INFO"] = "\xed\xa0\xbd"  # Surrogate pair in latin-1.
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        response = handler(environ, lambda *a, **k: None)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)

    def test_non_unicode_query_string(self):
        """
        Regression test for #20284.
        """
        environ = self.request_factory.get("/").environ
        environ["QUERY_STRING"] = "q=1&q=2"
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        response = handler(environ, lambda *a, **k: None)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)

"""
Testing using the Test Client

The test client is a class that can act like a simple
browser for testing purposes.

"""

import copy
import itertools
import tempfile
from unittest import mock

from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.core import mail
from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseNotAllowed
from django.test import (
    AsyncRequestFactory,
    Client,
    TestRequestBuilder,
    SimpleTestCase,
    TestCase,
    modify_settings,
    override_settings,
)
from django.urls import reverse_lazy
from django.utils.decorators import async_only_middleware
from django.views.generic import RedirectView


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="test_client.urls")
class TestRequestBuilderTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests for the test request builder."""

    # A mapping between names of HTTP/1.1 methods and their test views.
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def test_request_builder(self):
        """The request builder implements all the HTTP/1.1 methods."""
        for method_name in ("get", "post", "put", "patch", "delete", "head", "options"):
            method = getattr(self.request_factory, method_name)
            request = method("/somewhere/")
            self.assertEqual(request.method, method_name.upper())

    def test_get_request_from_factory(self):
        """
        The request builder returns a WSGIRequest for a GET request.
        """
        request = self.request_factory.get("/somewhere/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")

    def test_request_builder_default_headers(self):
        request = TestRequestBuilder(
            headers={
                "authorization": "Bearer faketoken",
                "x-another-header": "some other value",
            }
        ).get("/somewhere/")
        self.assertEqual(request.headers["authorization"], "Bearer faketoken")
        self.assertIn("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", request.META)
        self.assertEqual(request.headers["x-another-header"], "some other value")
        self.assertIn("HTTP_X_ANOTHER_HEADER", request.META)

    def test_request_builder_query_params(self):
        tests = (
            "get",
            "post",
            "put",
            "patch",
            "delete",
            "head",
            "options",
            "trace",
        )
        for method in tests:
            with self.subTest(method=method):
                factory = getattr(self.request_factory, method)
                request = factory("/somewhere", query_params={"example": "data"})
                self.assertEqual(request.GET["example"], "data")


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="test_client.urls")
class AsyncRequestFactoryTest(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = AsyncRequestFactory()

from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.core.servers.basehttp import get_internal_wsgi_application
from django.core.signals import request_started
from django.core.wsgi import get_wsgi_application
from django.db import close_old_connections
from django.http import FileResponse
from django.test import SimpleTestCase, override_settings
from django.test.client import TestRequestBuilder


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="wsgi.urls")
class WSGITest(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def setUp(self):
        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)
        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)

    def test_get_wsgi_application(self):
        """
        get_wsgi_application() returns a functioning WSGI callable.
        """
        application = get_wsgi_application()

        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/", CONTENT_TYPE="text/html; charset=utf-8", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )

        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)

        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertEqual(
            set(response_data["headers"]),
            {("Content-Length", "12"), ("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")},
        )
        self.assertIn(
            bytes(response),
            [
                b"Content-Length: 12\r\nContent-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\n\r\nHello World!",
                b"Content-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\nContent-Length: 12\r\n\r\nHello World!",
            ],
        )

    def test_wsgi_cookies(self):
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/cookie/", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )
        application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertIn(("Set-Cookie", "key=value; Path=/"), response_data["headers"])

    def test_file_wrapper(self):
        """
        FileResponse uses wsgi.file_wrapper.
        """

        class FileWrapper:
            def __init__(self, filelike, block_size=None):
                self.block_size = block_size
                filelike.close()

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/file/",
            REQUEST_METHOD="GET",
            **{"wsgi.file_wrapper": FileWrapper},
        )
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertIsInstance(response, FileWrapper)
        self.assertEqual(response.block_size, FileResponse.block_size)


class GetInternalWSGIApplicationTest(SimpleTestCase):
    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.wsgi.application")
    def test_success(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is a dotted path, the referenced object is
        returned.
        """
        app = get_internal_wsgi_application()

        from .wsgi import application

        self.assertIs(app, application)

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION=None)
    def test_default(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is ``None``, the return value of
        ``get_wsgi_application`` is returned.
        """
        # Mock out get_wsgi_application so we know its return value is used
        fake_app = object()

        def mock_get_wsgi_app():
            return fake_app

        from django.core.servers import basehttp

        _orig_get_wsgi_app = basehttp.get_wsgi_application
        basehttp.get_wsgi_application = mock_get_wsgi_app

        try:
            app = get_internal_wsgi_application()

            self.assertIs(app, fake_app)
        finally:
            basehttp.get_wsgi_application = _orig_get_wsgi_app

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.noexist.app")
    def test_bad_module(self):
        msg = "WSGI application 'wsgi.noexist.app' could not be loaded; Error importing"
        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ImproperlyConfigured, msg):
            get_internal_wsgi_application()

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.wsgi.noexist")
    def test_bad_name(self):
        msg = (
            "WSGI application 'wsgi.wsgi.noexist' could not be loaded; Error importing"
        )
        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ImproperlyConfigured, msg):
            get_internal_wsgi_application()

"""
Tests verifying that RequestFactory has been renamed to TestRequestBuilder.
"""
import django
from django.test import SimpleTestCase


class TestRequestBuilderImportTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests that TestRequestBuilder can be imported from the correct locations."""

    def test_import_from_django_test(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can be imported from django.test."""
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertIsNotNone(TestRequestBuilder)

    def test_import_from_django_test_client(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can be imported from django.test.client."""
        from django.test.client import TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertIsNotNone(TestRequestBuilder)

    def test_class_name_is_test_request_builder(self):
        """The class name should be TestRequestBuilder."""
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertEqual(TestRequestBuilder.__name__, "TestRequestBuilder")

    def test_backward_compat_alias(self):
        """RequestFactory should still work as an alias for TestRequestBuilder."""
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder, RequestFactory
        self.assertIs(TestRequestBuilder, RequestFactory)

    def test_in_all_django_test(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder should be in django.test.__all__."""
        import django.test
        self.assertIn("TestRequestBuilder", django.test.__all__)

    def test_in_all_django_test_client(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder should be in django.test.client.__all__."""
        import django.test.client
        self.assertIn("TestRequestBuilder", django.test.client.__all__)

    def test_request_factory_still_in_all(self):
        """RequestFactory alias should still be in __all__ for backward compat."""
        import django.test
        self.assertIn("RequestFactory", django.test.__all__)


class TestRequestBuilderFunctionalTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests that TestRequestBuilder works correctly."""

    def setUp(self):
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.rf = TestRequestBuilder()

    def test_get_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create GET requests."""
        request = self.rf.get("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")
        self.assertEqual(request.path, "/test/")

    def test_post_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create POST requests."""
        request = self.rf.post("/test/", {"key": "value"})
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "POST")

    def test_put_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create PUT requests."""
        request = self.rf.put("/test/", data="test", content_type="text/plain")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "PUT")

    def test_delete_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create DELETE requests."""
        request = self.rf.delete("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "DELETE")

    def test_patch_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create PATCH requests."""
        request = self.rf.patch("/test/", data="test", content_type="text/plain")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "PATCH")

    def test_head_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create HEAD requests."""
        request = self.rf.head("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "HEAD")

    def test_options_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create OPTIONS requests."""
        request = self.rf.options("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "OPTIONS")

    def test_secure_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create secure requests."""
        request = self.rf.get("/test/", secure=True)
        self.assertEqual(request.META["wsgi.url_scheme"], "https")

    def test_with_query_params(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create requests with query params."""
        request = self.rf.get("/test/", {"key": "value"})
        self.assertIn("key=value", request.META["QUERY_STRING"])

    def test_with_json_data(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create requests with JSON data."""
        import json
        request = self.rf.post(
            "/test/",
            data={"key": "value"},
            content_type="application/json",
        )
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "POST")
        self.assertIn("application/json", request.META["CONTENT_TYPE"])


class AsyncRequestFactoryTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests that AsyncRequestFactory still uses TestRequestBuilder as parent."""

    def test_async_request_factory_parent(self):
        """AsyncRequestFactory should inherit from TestRequestBuilder."""
        from django.test.client import AsyncRequestFactory, TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertTrue(issubclass(AsyncRequestFactory, TestRequestBuilder))

    def test_client_parent(self):
        """Client should inherit from TestRequestBuilder."""
        from django.test.client import Client, TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertTrue(issubclass(Client, TestRequestBuilder))


class TestRequestBuilderUsageTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests using TestRequestBuilder directly as class attribute."""

    request_factory = None  # Will be set in setUp

    def setUp(self):
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def test_class_attribute_usage(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can be used as a class attribute."""
        request = self.request_factory.get("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")

from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.core.servers.basehttp import get_internal_wsgi_application
from django.core.signals import request_started
from django.core.wsgi import get_wsgi_application
from django.db import close_old_connections
from django.http import FileResponse
from django.test import SimpleTestCase, override_settings
from django.test.client import TestRequestBuilder


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="wsgi.urls")
class WSGITest(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def setUp(self):
        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)
        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)

    def test_get_wsgi_application(self):
        """
        get_wsgi_application() returns a functioning WSGI callable.
        """
        application = get_wsgi_application()

        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/", CONTENT_TYPE="text/html; charset=utf-8", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )

        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)

        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertEqual(
            set(response_data["headers"]),
            {("Content-Length", "12"), ("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")},
        )
        self.assertIn(
            bytes(response),
            [
                b"Content-Length: 12\r\nContent-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\n\r\nHello World!",
                b"Content-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\nContent-Length: 12\r\n\r\nHello World!",
            ],
        )

    def test_wsgi_cookies(self):
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/cookie/", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )
        application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertIn(("Set-Cookie", "key=value; Path=/"), response_data["headers"])

    def test_file_wrapper(self):
        """
        FileResponse uses wsgi.file_wrapper.
        """

        class FileWrapper:
            def __init__(self, filelike, block_size=None):
                self.block_size = block_size
                filelike.close()

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/file/",
            REQUEST_METHOD="GET",
            **{"wsgi.file_wrapper": FileWrapper},
        )
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertIsInstance(response, FileWrapper)
        self.assertEqual(response.block_size, FileResponse.block_size)


class GetInternalWSGIApplicationTest(SimpleTestCase):
    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.wsgi.application")
    def test_success(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is a dotted path, the referenced object is
        returned.
        """
        app = get_internal_wsgi_application()

        from .wsgi import application

        self.assertIs(app, application)

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION=None)
    def test_default(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is ``None``, the return value of
        ``get_wsgi_application`` is returned.
        """
        # Mock out get_wsgi_application so we know its return value is used
        fake_app = object()

        def mock_get_wsgi_app():
            return fake_app

from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.core.servers.basehttp import get_internal_wsgi_application
from django.core.signals import request_started
from django.core.wsgi import get_wsgi_application
from django.db import close_old_connections
from django.http import FileResponse
from django.test import SimpleTestCase, override_settings
from django.test.client import TestRequestBuilder


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="wsgi.urls")
class WSGITest(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def setUp(self):
        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)
        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)

    def test_get_wsgi_application(self):
        """
        get_wsgi_application() returns a functioning WSGI callable.
        """
        application = get_wsgi_application()

        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/", CONTENT_TYPE="text/html; charset=utf-8", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )

        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)

        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertEqual(
            set(response_data["headers"]),
            {("Content-Length", "12"), ("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")},
        )
        self.assertIn(
            bytes(response),
            [
                b"Content-Length: 12\r\nContent-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\n\r\nHello World!",
                b"Content-Type: text/html; "
                b"charset=utf-8\r\nContent-Length: 12\r\n\r\nHello World!",
            ],
        )

    def test_wsgi_cookies(self):
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/cookie/", REQUEST_METHOD="GET"
        )
        application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertIn(("Set-Cookie", "key=value; Path=/"), response_data["headers"])

    def test_file_wrapper(self):
        """
        FileResponse uses wsgi.file_wrapper.
        """

        class FileWrapper:
            def __init__(self, filelike, block_size=None):
                self.block_size = block_size
                filelike.close()

        application = get_wsgi_application()
        environ = self.request_factory._base_environ(
            PATH_INFO="/file/",
            REQUEST_METHOD="GET",
            **{"wsgi.file_wrapper": FileWrapper},
        )
        response_data = {}

        def start_response(status, headers):
            response_data["status"] = status
            response_data["headers"] = headers

        response = application(environ, start_response)
        self.assertEqual(response_data["status"], "200 OK")
        self.assertIsInstance(response, FileWrapper)
        self.assertEqual(response.block_size, FileResponse.block_size)


class GetInternalWSGIApplicationTest(SimpleTestCase):
    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.wsgi.application")
    def test_success(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is a dotted path, the referenced object is
        returned.
        """
        app = get_internal_wsgi_application()

        from .wsgi import application

        self.assertIs(app, application)

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION=None)
    def test_default(self):
        """
        If ``WSGI_APPLICATION`` is ``None``, the return value of
        ``get_wsgi_application`` is returned.
        """
        # Mock out get_wsgi_application so we know its return value is used
        fake_app = object()

        def mock_get_wsgi_app():
            return fake_app

        from django.core.servers import basehttp

        _orig_get_wsgi_app = basehttp.get_wsgi_application
        basehttp.get_wsgi_application = mock_get_wsgi_app

        try:
            app = get_internal_wsgi_application()

            self.assertIs(app, fake_app)
        finally:
            basehttp.get_wsgi_application = _orig_get_wsgi_app

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.noexist.app")
    def test_bad_module(self):
        msg = "WSGI application 'wsgi.noexist.app' could not be loaded; Error importing"
        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ImproperlyConfigured, msg):
            get_internal_wsgi_application()

    @override_settings(WSGI_APPLICATION="wsgi.wsgi.noexist")
    def test_bad_name(self):
        msg = (
            "WSGI application 'wsgi.wsgi.noexist' could not be loaded; Error importing"
        )
        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ImproperlyConfigured, msg):
            get_internal_wsgi_application()

from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.core.handlers.wsgi import WSGIHandler, WSGIRequest, get_script_name
from django.core.signals import request_finished, request_started
from django.db import close_old_connections, connection
from django.test import (
    AsyncRequestFactory,
    TestRequestBuilder,
    SimpleTestCase,
    TransactionTestCase,
    override_settings,
)


class HandlerTests(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def setUp(self):
        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)
        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)

    def test_middleware_initialized(self):
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        self.assertIsNotNone(handler._middleware_chain)

    def test_bad_path_info(self):
        """
        A non-UTF-8 path populates PATH_INFO with an URL-encoded path and
        produces a 404.
        """
        environ = self.request_factory.get("/").environ
        environ["PATH_INFO"] = "\xed\xa0\xbd"  # Surrogate pair in latin-1.
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        response = handler(environ, lambda *a, **k: None)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)

    def test_non_unicode_query_string(self):
        """
        Regression test for #20284.
        """
        environ = self.request_factory.get("/").environ
        environ["QUERY_STRING"] = "q=1&q=2"
        handler = WSGIHandler()
        response = handler(environ, lambda *a, **k: None)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)
PYEOF

import asyncio
import os
from inspect import iscoroutinefunction
from unittest import mock

from asgiref.sync import async_to_sync

from django.core.cache import DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS, caches
from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, SynchronousOnlyOperation
from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseNotAllowed
from django.test import TestRequestBuilder, SimpleTestCase
from django.utils.asyncio import async_unsafe
from django.views.generic.base import View


class CacheTest(SimpleTestCase):
    def test_caches_local(self):
        @async_to_sync
        async def async_cache():
            return caches[DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS]

        cache_1 = async_cache()
        cache_2 = async_cache()
        self.assertIs(cache_1, cache_2)


class AsyncUnsafeTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """
    async_unsafe decorator should work correctly and returns the correct
    message.
    """

    @async_unsafe
    def dangerous_method(self):
        return True

    async def test_async_unsafe(self):
        msg = (
            "You cannot call this from an async context - use a thread or "
            "sync_to_async."
        )
        with self.assertRaisesMessage(SynchronousOnlyOperation, msg):
            self.dangerous_method()


class HttpMethodNotAllowedTest(SimpleTestCase):
    def test_http_method_not_allowed_responds_correctly(self):
        request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()
        tests = [
            (HttpResponse, "GET"),
            (HttpResponseNotAllowed, "POST"),
        ]
        for response_class, method in tests:
            with self.subTest(response_class=response_class, method=method):
                request = getattr(request_factory, method.lower())("/test/")
                view = lambda r: response_class("test")
                response = view(request)
                self.assertEqual(type(response), response_class)

from django.test import SimpleTestCase


class TestRequestBuilderImportTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests that TestRequestBuilder can be imported from the correct locations."""

        """TestRequestBuilder can be imported from django.test."""
        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.assertIsNotNone(TestRequestBuilder)

        from django.test.client import TestRequestBuilder

        self.assertEqual(TestRequestBuilder.__name__, "TestRequestBuilder")

    def test_backward_compat_alias(self):

        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder, RequestFactory
        self.assertIs(TestRequestBuilder, RequestFactory)

    def test_in_all_django_test(self):

        import django.test
        self.assertIn("TestRequestBuilder", django.test.__all__)

    def test_in_all_django_test_client(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder should be in django.test.client.__all__."""
        import django.test.client
        self.assertIn("TestRequestBuilder", django.test.client.__all__)

    def test_request_factory_still_in_all(self):
        """RequestFactory alias should still be in __all__ for backward compat."""
        import django.test
        self.assertIn("RequestFactory", django.test.__all__)

        from django.test import TestRequestBuilder
        self.rf = TestRequestBuilder()

        request = self.rf.get("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")
        self.assertEqual(request.path, "/test/")

        """TestRequestBuilder can create POST requests."""
        request = self.rf.post("/test/", {"key": "value"})
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "POST")

    def test_put_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create PUT requests."""
        request = self.rf.put("/test/", data="test", content_type="text/plain")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "PUT")

    def test_delete_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create DELETE requests."""
        request = self.rf.delete("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "DELETE")

    def test_patch_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create PATCH requests."""
        request = self.rf.patch("/test/", data="test", content_type="text/plain")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "PATCH")

    def test_head_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create HEAD requests."""
        request = self.rf.head("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "HEAD")

    def test_options_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create OPTIONS requests."""
        request = self.rf.options("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "OPTIONS")

    def test_secure_request(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create secure requests."""
        request = self.rf.get("/test/", secure=True)
        self.assertEqual(request.META["wsgi.url_scheme"], "https")

        """TestRequestBuilder can create requests with query params."""
        request = self.rf.get("/test/", {"key": "value"})
        self.assertIn("key=value", request.META["QUERY_STRING"])

    def test_with_json_data(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can create requests with JSON data."""

        request = self.rf.post(

            content_type="application/json",

        self.assertIn("application/json", request.META["CONTENT_TYPE"])

        self.assertTrue(issubclass(Client, TestRequestBuilder))

    def test_class_attribute_usage(self):
        """TestRequestBuilder can be used as a class attribute."""
        request = self.request_factory.get("/test/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")

"""
Testing using the Test Client

The test client is a class that can act like a simple
browser for testing purposes.

"""

import copy
import itertools
import tempfile
from unittest import mock

from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.core import mail
from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseNotAllowed
from django.test import (
    AsyncRequestFactory,
    Client,
    TestRequestBuilder,
    SimpleTestCase,
    TestCase,
    modify_settings,
    override_settings,
)
from django.urls import reverse_lazy
from django.utils.decorators import async_only_middleware
from django.views.generic import RedirectView


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="test_client.urls")
class TestRequestBuilderTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests for the test request builder."""

    # A mapping between names of HTTP/1.1 methods and their test views.
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

    def test_request_builder(self):
        """The request builder implements all the HTTP/1.1 methods."""
        for method_name in ("get", "post", "put", "patch", "delete", "head", "options"):
            method = getattr(self.request_factory, method_name)
            request = method("/somewhere/")
            self.assertEqual(request.method, method_name.upper())

    def test_get_request_from_factory(self):
        """
        The request builder returns a WSGIRequest for a GET request.
        """
        request = self.request_factory.get("/somewhere/")
        self.assertEqual(request.method, "GET")

    def test_request_builder_default_headers(self):
        request = TestRequestBuilder(
            headers={
                "authorization": "Bearer faketoken",
                "x-another-header": "some other value",
            }
        ).get("/somewhere/")
        self.assertEqual(request.headers["authorization"], "Bearer faketoken")
        self.assertIn("HTTP_AUTHORIZATION", request.META)
        self.assertEqual(request.headers["x-another-header"], "some other value")
        self.assertIn("HTTP_X_ANOTHER_HEADER", request.META)

    def test_request_builder_query_params(self):
        tests = (
            "get",
            "post",
            "put",
            "patch",
            "delete",
            "head",
            "options",
            "trace",
        )
        for method in tests:
            with self.subTest(method=method):
                factory = getattr(self.request_factory, method)
                request = factory("/somewhere", query_params={"example": "data"})
                self.assertEqual(request.GET["example"], "data")


@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF="test_client.urls")
class AsyncRequestFactoryTest(SimpleTestCase):
    request_factory = AsyncRequestFactory()
